title: Enforce a tool-surface budget and operation-catalog gate
goal: Prevent unexamined Top-Level-Tool growth by requiring each added public tool to prove a distinct authority boundary, materially different call shape or measured selection advantage; otherwise model the capability as a named typed operation behind an existing entry point.

acceptance.assertion: The repository records a generated baseline of public tool names, categories, authority classes and operation-catalog entries bound to the runtime contract.
Validation fails when a new public tool lacks a machine-readable justification naming the distinct authority boundary, materially different argument schema or measured tool-choice advantage.
New capabilities without an accepted exception are represented as named operations with typed parameters, explicit effects, validation and rollback metadata rather than unconstrained strings.

Irreversible deletion, secret reveal/use/export, process signalling, privileged actions and other materially different authority or reversibility boundaries are not collapsed merely to reduce the count.
The gate does not enforce an unexplained fixed maximum. It reports growth, accepted exceptions, retired entries and operation migration candidates.
Existing clients retain stable tool names unless a separately reviewed deprecation and compatibility path exists.
Tests demonstrate one rejected unnecessary tool, one accepted security-boundary tool and one capability successfully added as a catalog operation.

metadata.risk_note: A generic operation dispatcher can reduce discoverability and blur authority unless operation schemas and effects remain explicit.
validation: Exact CI command make validate passed with the bare system Python: 1691 tests passed, 1 expected skip.
GitHub Actions validate passed on Python 3.10 and 3.12 for final head 5d3f4b4d73a5212bcd8fdb43ee87cb8c16755f65.
Ruff passed for the new validator and tests; a mutation corpus matched the full Draft 2020-12 jsonschema validator.
The head- and diff-bound four-pass self-review returned PASS with no material findings and uncertainty 0.08.
Post-merge self-deploy completed as release 20260715T044254Z-9f21b14a78d9 with complete and integrity-valid finalization.
Live tool-surface check on canonical main reported valid=true, baseline_tool_count=125, current_tool_count=125, growth=0, operation_count=3 and schema_valid=true.
metadata.outcome: Grabowski now freezes the current 125-tool public surface with code-bound name and semantic hashes, rejects unbudgeted additions and baseline grandfathering, requires machine-readable exceptions for genuinely distinct top-level tools, and defaults ordinary capability growth to typed named operations with explicit execution authorities, effects and reversibility. No existing public tool name or security boundary was collapsed.

verified_acceptance.surface-baseline: The repository records 125 public tools with category, derived authority class, risk, read-only state, effects and reversibility, bound to runtime-entrypoint and capability-catalog hashes; three operation entries are separately projected and hashed.
verified_acceptance.merge-gate: make validate invokes the budget gate. Tests reject an unbudgeted tool, capability-semantic drift, schema drift and an attempted baseline rewrite with recomputed hashes.
verified_acceptance.operation-default: inspect-user-service was added as a typed operation with a bounded unit regex, fixed argv shape, service-read effect, operator-mutation execution authority and not-applicable reversibility, without adding a public MCP tool.
verified_acceptance.security-boundaries-preserved: The policy explicitly permits reviewed security-boundary tools and the positive exception test accepts a separately authorized secret-rotation surface rather than forcing it behind a generic dispatcher.
verified_acceptance.no-arbitrary-cap: fixed_tool_cap remains null. The report exposes baseline/current counts, growth, accepted additions, retirements and migration candidates.
verified_acceptance.compatibility: All 125 existing public tool names remain unchanged; operation schema v1 execution remains compatible and src/grabowski_operations.py was not modified.
verified_acceptance.proof: Focused tests prove rejection of an unnecessary tool, acceptance of a distinct security-boundary tool and addition of a typed operation; full local, CI, deployment and live readback are green.
